title: Council of Families
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Organization |image=[[File:ChissFemales-FF137.png]] |name=Council of Families |type=Legislative{{CSWECite|I|148|Council of Families}} |founder= |leader= |subgroup= |headquarters= |locations=[[Ac'siel]], [[Csilla/Legends|Csilla]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'' |formed= |founded= |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation=[[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] }} {{Quote|Or I return after they're hidden and negotiate in secret with the Council of Families for their safe passage home. Even if such negotiations take a few months, the survivors will at least have a habitable world to live on.|Syndic Mitth'ras'safis|[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']]}} The '''Council of Families''' was the primary legislative body of the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] formed from represenatives of the [[Ruling Families/Legends|Ruling Families]]. The Council ensured that each Ruling [[Chiss family/Legends|Family]] remained on equal footing. ==Description== {{Quote|Just as our families are defined by their function in society, so are we defined by our roles as representatives of those families. We are here before you not as people, but as the beginning and end points of a decision-making process.|Bronze, to Mara Jade Skywalker|}} The Council of Families was the primary legislative body of the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] that was composed of represenatives of the [[Ruling Families/Legends|Ruling Families]]. The Council was given certain judicial responsibilities as a way to govern the activities of individuals and Ruling [[Chiss family/Legends|Families]], ensuring that each Family remained on equal footing with the others.{{CSWECite|I|148|Council of Families}} If there were ever a deadlock over a decision among the Council, the deciding vote fell on the representative from the [[Chiss Expansionary Defense Force|Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet]]. Its members wore head-to-foot [[Chiss robes of state|robes]] in the color of the Family they represented that concealed their identities. ==History== ===Galactic Republic=== {{Quote|He can't do that. Can he?"''
"''Not legally. But as a practical matter, if he's brought enough vessels, there'll be no way for us to stop him."''
''"The Council of Families—"''
''"—will certainly object. But the procedure will be long and complex.|Syndic Mitth'ras'safis and Admiral Ar'alani|}} [[File:OutboundFlight-SotGSE.png|thumb|left|300px|Admiral Ar'alani and Syndic Mitth'ras'safis believed the Council of Families would object if the Fifth Ruling Family took possession of the damaged starship ''Outbound Flight'' (pictured).]] After a force of [[Warship/Legends|warships]] [[Commanding officer/Legends|led]] by [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]], [[Core name/Legends|core name]] "Thrawn," of the [[Chiss Expansionary Fleet/Legends|Chiss Expansionary Fleet]] [[Battle for Outbound Flight|crippled]] the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Scout ship/Legends|exploration]] and [[Colony ship/Legends|colonization vessel]] ''[[Outbound Flight]]'' in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] to [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. a Chiss [[Chaf'orm'bintrano's fleet|fleet]] led by [[Aristocra/Legends|Aristocra]] [[Chaf'orm'bintrano]] of the [[Chaf family/Legends|Fifth Ruling Family]] was approaching the [[Unidentified Unknown Regions star system (red sun)|battle site]]. Informed by a suggestion of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[Jorj Car'das]], Thrawn became convinced that the Aristocra would attempt to claim possession of the damaged ''Outbound Flight'' for the Fifth Family. [[Syndic/Legends|Syndic]] [[Mitth'ras'safis/Legends|Mitth'ras'safis]], core name "Thrass," of the [[Mitth family/Legends|Eighth Ruling Family]] and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Ar'alani/Legends|Ar'alani]] of the [[Chiss Defense Fleet]] believed that the Council of Families would object if a Ruling Family was acting to upset the balance of power, by forcibly taking the technology present aboard the ship to give their forces an advantage over the other Families. However, this procedure was noted for being long and complex, which was largely ineffective in practical considerations, agreeing that the best course of action would be to instead have the ''Outbound Flight'' be declared Defense Fleet property at [[Pesfavri/Legends|one of its bases]]. In reality, however, Thrass planned to scuttle the vessel in a nearby [[Star/Legends|star]]. As Thrass and Car'das proceeded with the plan, they encountered [[Lorana Jinzler]], a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who was one among a small group of the ''Outbound Flight''{{'s}} crewmembers and passengers who had survived the Chiss attack. After realizing that evacuating and destroying the ship before it was captured by the Fifth Family was impossible, Thrass suggested that they follow Thrawn's original plan of taking the vessel to a Defense Fleet base. However, they realized that such a course of action would effectively render the survivors of the ''Outbound Flight'' captives of the Chiss. Instead, Thrass provided them with the information about a safe location inside a [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]] known to Chiss as [[The Redoubt/Legends|the Redoubt]], where they could stay out of sight while Thrass would return to secretly negotiate with the Council of Families about ''Outbound Flight''{{'s}} return to the Republic. Following Jinzler's order, Car'das stayed behind while Thrass and the Jedi took the ship to its final voyage into the star cluster. ===Galactic Alliance=== {{Quote|So we find ourselves at an impasse,''"
"''A tie,''"
"''This is not an uncommon situation, given the diversity of our needs.''"
"''As in all such situations, we turn to the Expansionary Defense Fleet to cast the deciding vote.|Bronze, Copper-green, Gray and Rust|}} [[File:Chisswomen.jpg|thumb|right|300px|"Bronze" and "Rust," [[Gender/Legends|female]] Chiss members of the Council of Families.]] In their [[Hunt for Zonama Sekot|search]] for the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'' to [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]]. a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] delegation led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] visited the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Ac'siel]] on the planet [[Csilla/Legends|Csilla]]. There, they met with the members of the Council of Families, "[[Bronze (Chiss)|Bronze]]," "[[Rust]]," "[[Gray (Chiss)|Gray]]" and "[[Copper-green]]" among them, who wore bronze, rust-red, silver-gray and copper-green head-to-foot [[Robe/Legends|robes]] that respectively reflected the colors of their Families. During their meeting, the representatives debated whether to permit the delegation to pass through the Chiss Space, with two members being open to the idea. [[Chief Navigator]] [[Peita Aabe]], a representative from the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet, cast the deciding vote in favor of a compromise because the Council was unable to reach consensus. With unanimous approval from the Council, Aabe granted the delegation access to the [[Expeditionary Library]] for two [[Standard day/Legends|days]], while Gray presented them with [[Tris (Chiss)|Tris]], a guide from the [[Inrokini family]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Council of Families first appeared unnamed in the [[2003]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'', the second book in [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']]{{'s}} [[Force Heretic trilogy|''Force Heretic'' trilogy]], written by [[Sean Williams]] and [[Shane Dix]]. It was first identified in the [[2006]] novel [[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] written by [[Timothy Zahn]]. ==Appearances== *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} {{1stID|Council of Families|in book}} *[[Outbound Flight (abridged audiobook)|''Outbound Flight'' abridged audiobook]] {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{FactFile|137|CHI 1-4|Chiss}} {{Po}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Rat der Familien |es=Consejo de Familias }} [[Category:Chiss Ascendancy organizations]] [[Category:Legislatures]]
